April 12, 2025
THE HON. FRANK BLAS, JR., Speaker
I Mina 'trentai Ocha Na Liheslaturan Guahan
38th Guam Legislature
Guam Congress Building
163 Chalan Santo Papa
Hagatfia, Guam 96910
Re: Bill No. 8-38 (COR), "AN ACT TO AMEND§ 5127(a) AND REPEAL§ 5127(d) OF
SUBARTICLE C, ARTICLE 2, CHAPTER 5, TITLE 5, GUAM CODE
ANNOTATED, RELATIVE TO THE EXTENSION OF GOVERNMENT OF
GUAM LEASES FROM FIVE (5) TO FIFTEEN (15) YEARS."
Hafa Adai Mr. Speaker,
Bill No. 8-38 (COR) increases the allowable period for commercial contracts, leases, permits, and
licenses for use of public real property and related facilities by non-governmental entities from
five (5) to fifteen (15) years. As stated in testimony provided by government agencies directly
involved in economic development, Bill 8-38 will make the leasing of government property more
attractive to developers who will invest in the development of leased government property and
facilities, which will in turn expand investment opportunities in our community and elevate
Guam's competitiveness in the region.
Bill 8-38 also removes the requirement of legislative approval of exceptional term contracts that
exceed the maximum number of years allowed, resulting in significant uncertainty, delays, and
procedural barriers that may have dissuaded potential investors and developers concerned about
the lack of a clear and predictable process not influenced by political cycles. Prolonged debate and
the possibility of inaction in the legislative process made it difficult for investors to commit to
major ventures and infrastructure projects in Guam when there are other competitive markets in
the region with more streamlined leasing frameworks.
This measure helps create a more business-friendly environment in Guam and is a step forward in
ensuring that private sector partners have the confidence to invest, grow, and thrive in our
economy.
II
RICARDO J. BORDALLO GOVERNOR'S COMPLEX
